steps.title: Find the Oscar pattern
steps.content: The example needs to show an Oscar-recognized film with a modest opening weekend but stronger lifetime earnings than a comparable film without that recognition.

steps.title: Reject incomplete comparisons
steps.content: Choice A gives only opening-weekend figures. Choice C compares two Oscar-recognized films, so it cannot show the effect of recognition against nonrecognition. Choice D points to a nonrecognized film earning more, weakening the journalist's claim. The remaining choice contrasts Amélie's weaker opening with its stronger lifetime earnings. The answer is B.
